title: Polynomial, Rational, and Nonlinear Models · Error Forensics
summary: Compare alternative growth and geometry models for a production process. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a nonlinear model comparison report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate polynomial degree, roots, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.
Fit and critique polynomial models without extrapolating unsupported behavior.
Interpret zeros, holes, asymptotes, and domain restrictions in rational models.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A model passes through (3, 11) and (7, 23). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Model Selection for a nonlinear model comparison report.
steps.why: The Model Selection result for a nonlinear model comparison report is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Model Selection, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a nonlinear model comparison report. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
